zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire於1203年出生在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯時，他的父親Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourne20歲，母親Isabel de Briwere19歲。他於1229年5月29日之前與Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham在英國越可爾可廷加姆結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他在英國被列為justiciar (chief justice)。他去世於1241年12月18日之前在以色列מחוז ירושליםיְרוּשָׁלַיִם，埋葬在巴勒斯坦巴勒斯坦耶路撒冷。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire于1203年出生在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾布利斯沃斯时，他的父亲Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourne20岁，母亲Isabel de Briwere19岁。他于1229年5月29日之前与Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham在英国越可爾可廷加姆结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他在英国被列为justiciar (chief justice)。他去世于1241年12月18日之前在以色列מחוז ירושליםיְרוּשָׁלַיִם，埋葬在巴勒斯坦巴勒斯坦耶路撒冷。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Wake
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

occupational name for ‘(man of) the watch’, from Middle English wak, wake ‘wake, vigil, watch, period of guard duty’ (Old English wacu, Old Norse vaka). It alternates with the synonymous Middle English wache (Old English wacce), hence the variant Watch. The surname was also interchangeable with Waite (the equivalent Old French term) which, like Wake, was sometimes translated as Latin vigil ‘(one who is) watchful, alert’. Compare the synonymous Vail . When the bearers had high social status, the surname probably denoted an officer with responsibility for the security of a castle, town, or royal person. Compare Wakeman .
variant of   Wack  .

variants.name: Vail
meanings: English: occupational name from Old French de la veille ‘of the watch, watchman’, with loss of de; compare Wake . There has been some confusion with the topographic name Vale and the nickname Veal .
Scottish: shortened and altered form of McPhail , Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Phàil ‘son of Paul’.
Irish: variant of   Veale  .

variants.name: Waite
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name for a watchman, either in a town or castle, from Anglo-Norman French waite ‘watchman, member of the watch’ (of ancient Germanic origin; compare Wachter ). In the Middle Ages the town waits typically combined the functions of watchmen and musicians through being trumpeters or pipers. There may also have been some late confusion with White or Wheat .

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire was born in 1203, in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourne, was 20 and his mother, Isabel de Briwere, was 19. He married Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham before 29 May 1229, in Cottingham, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. His occupation is listed as justiciar (chief justice) in England. He died before 18 December 1241, in Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israel, and was buried in Jerusalem, West Bank, Palestine.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire est né en 1203, à Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourne, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Isabel de Briwere, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham avant 29 mai 1229, à Cottingham, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Sa profession de son indiquée est justiciar (chief justice) à Angleterre. Il est décédé avant 18 décembre 1241, à Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israël, et a été enterré à Jerusalem, Cisjordanie, Palestine.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire 1203 in Blisworth, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourne 20 und seine Mutter Isabel de Briwere 19. Er hat vor 29. Mai 1229 in Cottingham, Yorkshire, England Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. sein Beruf ist als justiciar (chief justice) in England aufgeführt. Er starb vor 18. Dezember 1241 in Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israel und wurde in Jerusalem, Westjordanland, Palästina begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire è nato il nel 1203 nel Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourne, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Isabel de Briwere, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham prima del 29 maggio 1229, nel Cottingham, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. La sua occupazione è indicata come justiciar (chief justice) nel England. Morì prima del 18 dicembre 1241, nel Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israele, e fu sepolto a Jerusalem, Cisgiordania, Palestina.

ja.lifeSummary: Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshireが1203年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブリスウオルスで生まれた時，父Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourneは20歳，母Isabel de Briwereは19歳であった。1229年5月29日以前にイギリスヨークシュアイアコッティンガムでJoan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottinghamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。イギリスでの職業はjusticiar (chief justice)と記録されている。彼は1241年12月18日以前にイスラエルמחוז ירושליםיְרוּשָׁלַיִםで死去し，パレスチナヨルダン川西岸地区ジャーウサレムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire(이)가 1203년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 블리스워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourne(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel de Briwere(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1229년 5월 29일 이전 영국 요르크실르 컷잉함에서 Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국에서 justiciar (chief justice)이었다. 그는 1241년 12월 18일 이전 이스라엘 מחוז ירושלים יְרוּשָׁלַיִם에서 사망했으며 팔레스타인 팔레스타인 제루사렘에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire nasceu em 1203, em Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourne, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Isabel de Briwere, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham antes 29 de maio de 1229, em Cottingham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Sua profissão aparece como justiciar (chief justice) em Inglaterra. Ele faleceu antes 18 de dezembro de 1241, em Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israel, e foi sepultado em Jerusalem, Cisjordânia, Palestina.

ru.Jerusalem, Kingdom of Jersualem: джирсеилем, Западный берег реки Иордан, Палестина
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire родился в 1203 г. в местности блисворт, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourne было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel de Briwere, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham перед 29 май 1229 г. в местности коттингхам, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь.  Он работал по профессии justiciar (chief justice) в местности Англия. Он умер перед 18 декабрь 1241 г. в местности יְרוּשָׁלַיִם, מחוז ירושלים, Израиль и был похоронен в местности джирсеилем, Западный берег реки Иордан, Палестина.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hugh le Wake Sheriff of Yorkshire nació en 1203, en Blisworth, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Baldwin Wake II, Lord of Bourne, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Isabel de Briwere, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Joan de Stuteville Heiress of Cottingham antes de 29 de mayo de 1229, en Cottingham, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Su ocupación registrada era justiciar (chief justice) en Inglaterra. Murió antes de 18 de diciembre de 1241, en Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israel, y fue sepultado en Jerusalem, Cisjordania, Palestina.
